bug_fix_report.reproduction: Replace the fixed 200 ms sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event; this consistently exposes a missing wake-up whenever the worker registers after the queue transition.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: The worker listener can register after the one-shot completion transition, causing the wake-up to be lost.
confirming_evidence: Every captured failure checked 8–47 ms before worker completion, while passing runs happened to complete within the fixed 200 ms sleep.
After condition-based stabilization, the missing wake-up occurred whenever worker registration followed the queue transition.
With listener registration moved before the transition, 500 of 500 stabilized runs passed.
Removing only the ordering fix caused the same missing wake-up to return within 12 runs.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Register the worker listener before the completion transition; preserve production behavior outside that ordering change, keep the condition-based test wait, and remove the fixed sleep.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Keep the condition-based completion-event test and hand it to pc-tdd; the stabilized check passed 500 consecutive runs with the fix.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The fixed 200 ms sleep allows the assertion to run before worker completion.
debug_journal.prediction: Failing runs will check before worker completion, while passing runs will complete within 200 ms.
debug_journal.experiment: Instrument event enqueue and worker completion timestamps without changing the wait.
debug_journal.result: All captured failures checked 8–47 ms before completion; passing runs completed within 200 ms.

debug_journal.hypothesis: A condition-based wait will remove timing luck and reveal whether the completion event is eventually emitted.
debug_journal.prediction: The race will become deterministic and expose any lost completion wake-up.
debug_journal.experiment: Replace only the fixed sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event, leaving production code unchanged.
debug_journal.result: The stabilized test reproduced a missing wake-up whenever worker registration occurred after the queue transition.

debug_journal.hypothesis: Registering the listener after the one-shot transition loses the wake-up.
debug_journal.prediction: Registering the listener before the transition will make the stabilized reproduction pass; removing only that ordering fix will restore the same failure.
debug_journal.experiment: Change only worker registration ordering, run 500 stabilized repetitions, then remove only the ordering fix and repeat.
debug_journal.result: The fix passed 500/500 runs; with only the fix removed, the same missing wake-up returned within 12 runs.
